title: The Dirge
content:
혻혻혻혻Out of the pregnant darkness, where from fire
혻혻혻혻To glimmering fire the watchword leaps,
혻혻혻혻The dirge floats up from those who build the pyre
혻혻혻혻High and still higher
혻혻혻혻That yet shall blaze across the verminous deeps.

혻혻혻혻Farewell, O brother-heart,
혻혻혻혻Yet we shall not forget;
혻혻혻혻Though hand from hand must part,
혻혻혻혻Your hope is with us yet.
혻혻혻혻The clank of the swaggerer占퐏 sword
혻혻혻혻And clink of the grasper占퐏 gold
혻혻혻혻Are not so loud as the lover占퐏 word
혻혻혻혻In a thousand echoes rolled.

혻혻혻혻The lords of the tottering order sit and plot,
혻혻혻혻With cunning courtesy haggling still:
혻혻혻혻The insistent chorus cannot be forgot
혻혻혻혻Its words are shot
혻혻혻혻Like summoning rockets from the eastern hill.

혻혻혻혻You, it was you who showed
혻혻혻혻How Murder made his pact
혻혻혻혻In busy Greed占퐏 abode,
혻혻혻혻Preparing for the act.
혻혻혻혻To save the fatherland
혻혻혻혻They bade your comrades die,
혻혻혻혻And full in their path you took your stand
혻혻혻혻To kill the patriot lie.

혻혻혻혻Now, lest their flags and bags be lost in flame.
혻혻혻혻The desperate pair have summoned those
혻혻혻혻Whose love is moderate and whose life is tame
혻혻혻혻To quench in shame
혻혻혻혻The light that streams where wind of warning blows.

혻혻혻혻The ranks of freedom swell,
혻혻혻혻The flag of love rolls out:
혻혻혻혻The efficient ranks of hell
혻혻혻혻Close up in deadly doubt.
혻혻혻혻Moulded in battle占퐏 mire,
혻혻혻혻The bullet found its mark;
혻혻혻혻A living spirit, winged with fire,
혻혻혻혻Flares homeward from the dark.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Le Gay Brereton represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of John Le Gay Brereton's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.
